message:
docs(proposal): add self-contained NATS integration architecture memo

Add `PROPOSAL_TO_INTEGRATE_IDEAS_FROM_NATS_INTO_ASUPERSYNC.md` as a
standalone design memo that can be handed to another model or reviewer
without requiring prior repo context.

The document does more than brainstorm "pub/sub in Rust". It frames a
much larger thesis: use NATS's subject-centric worldview, topology
instincts, and consumer/stream ideas as raw material for an
Asupersync-native messaging fabric built on regions, cancellation
protocols, obligations, capabilities, replay, and FrankenSuite
decision/evidence surfaces.

Major content included in this commit:

- self-contained background sections explaining what Asupersync is,
  how `Cx`, regions, cancellation, obligations, replay, and RaptorQ fit
  together, and what gives the project its distinctive correctness model
- a self-contained NATS background covering subjects, queue groups,
  JetStream, accounts/import-export, gateways, leaf nodes, and why NATS
  is operationally compelling
- a common native-fabric plan for subject routing, durable stream
  capture, consumer policy, control-plane subjects, and NATS edge
  compatibility without letting NATS semantics distort the internal
  runtime model
- a brokerless peer-cooperative direction based on canonical
  `SubjectCell` ownership, control capsules, leases, stewardship
  handoff, delegated reads, and RaptorQ-backed recoverable mobility
- an explicit inversion section explaining what Asupersync can derive
  structurally from its concurrency substrate that NATS cannot obtain
  from a goroutine-and-broker foundation even with similar surface
  features
- several ambitious extensions beyond conventional messaging,
  including recoverable service capsules, contract-carrying services,
  protocol-carrying subjects, branch-addressable reality, causality-
  native views, semantic execution-lane sharding above canonical cells,
  budget-carrying traffic, and a retention-governed certified cut index
- a phased implementation plan that treats the subject-cell/brokerless
  work as first-class plan elements rather than a fenced-off research
  appendix
- repeated soundness-frontier guardrails so the proposal does not claim
  magical exactly-once semantics, unrestricted time travel, arbitrary
  semantic inference, or guarantees outside the modeled Asupersync-
  hosted boundary

The proposal was repeatedly tightened to remove overclaims, sequence the
phases correctly, distinguish ephemeral traffic from durable capture,
clarify provider-vs-caller authority, define replay and restore limits,
and make retention/access-control requirements explicit for cuts,
branches, witness storage, and delegated serving.
